comics.title: Pride and Prejudice
comics.source: Pride and Prejudice by Jane Austen
comics.description: Jane Austen's 1813 novel of manners, wit, and misjudged first impressions, in which Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy talk their way slowly into love.

comics.title: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
comics.source: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe by C.S. Lewis
comics.description: C.S. Lewis's 1950 fantasy in which four English children step through a wardrobe into the snow-bound land of Narnia, where a great lion named Aslan is set against the White Witch's endless winter.

comics.title: Sense and Sensibility
comics.source: Sense and Sensibility by Jane Austen
comics.description: Jane Austen's 1811 debut, following the Dashwood sisters — practical Elinor and passionate Marianne — through grief, courtship, and the slow, uneven shaping of a true marriage of heart and mind.

comics.title: Treasure Island
comics.source: Treasure Island by Robert Louis Stevenson
comics.description: Robert Louis Stevenson's 1883 adventure in which young Jim Hawkins sets sail in search of buried pirate gold and meets, in the charming and dangerous Long John Silver, the most memorable rogue in English literature.

comics.title: Northanger Abbey
comics.description: Jane Austen's posthumously published 1817 satire of Gothic novels, in which young Catherine Morland's imagination runs wild at an old English abbey — until life teaches her that real love is gentler, and stranger, than fiction.

comics.title: Hamlet
comics.description: Shakespeare's c. 1600 tragedy of a Danish prince summoned by his father's ghost to avenge a murder — a long, unsteady meditation on action, doubt, mortality, and the terrible weight of knowing.

comics.title: Jane Eyre
comics.description: Charlotte Brontë's 1847 Gothic romance of conscience and self-respect, following orphaned Jane from hardship and loneliness toward love, moral courage, and a home of her own.
